title: Lyra
tagline: You can't trust anyone these days.

intro.text: Dense jungle terrain. Light through canopy, unhurried pace. Duke travels alone, relaxed.
Distant sounds ahead — voices, movement, something unsettled. Not nature. He slows, reads it, and quietly adjusts his path to give it distance. Doesn't investigate.

Mid-step — incoming movement in his peripheral. Without breaking stride, he raises one hand and deflects the arrow with the back of his wrist. No stance. No announcement. Just a reflex that shouldn't be possible at that speed.
Through the trees — a woman at distance, bow raised, already nocking a second arrow. She's watching him with sharp focus. Not panic. Not a warning shot. She read him as a threat and acted on it.
She doesn't shout. She fires again.

intro.speaker: Duke
intro.text: I don't know what your problem is — I was only passing by. But if you keep this up, I'll have no choice but to defend myself.
Her expression doesn't change. She fires again.

outro.text: She pauses. Draws Toll deliberately. An arrow forms from red ice — crystalline, strange-coloured.

Duke catches it — and his hand freezes on contact, ice spreading up his fingers. He shatters it with his other hand. The break releases heat — a burn blooms across his palm. He pulls water Toll through the hand. Negates it. Looks at his palm for half a second.
Then looks at her. Neither speaks. Both have seen the other's ceiling — and neither knows where it actually ends.

intro.text: Duke moves. Controlled, reading her firing intervals, closing before she can adjust. He's inside her range.
She's better here than expected. Fast, clean technique. She buys herself space twice.

outro.text: He works her into position, deflects her last strike, and takes her to the ground — pinning both wrists. She fights the pin for a few seconds. Then stops — not defeated, recalculating.

outro.speaker: Duke
outro.text: I told you. I have nothing to do with anyone here. Let's stop this.
She holds his gaze. He had every opportunity to hurt her. He didn't take any of them. Something in her expression shifts — not trust, but recalibration.
Later, on the road. Side by side but not close — Duke brushing dirt off his robes, Lyra straightening her gloves, checking her bow for damage.

outro.speaker: Duke
outro.text: Soooo... why did you attack me in the first place?

outro.speaker: Lyra
outro.text: Bandits come through this area. They target the village nearby — and me. You moved like one of them.

outro.speaker: Duke
outro.text: So you thought I was one of them?

outro.speaker: Lyra
outro.text: You can't trust anyone these days.

outro.speaker: Lyra
outro.text: As an apology for mistaking you for a bandit... nice to meet someone nice for a change. My name is Lyra. What's yours?
